253: function evaluateContext(signals, gaps, contradictions) {
254:   const thresholds = {
255:     sliceCoverage: 0.7,
256:     reuseCatalogCoverage: 0.6,
257:     agreementRate: 0.5,
258:     relevanceFloor: 0.5,
259:     dependencyCompleteness: 0.7,
260:   };
261:   const blockers = [];
262:   const trace = [
263:     { signal: 'sliceCoverage', value: signals.sliceCoverage, threshold: thresholds.sliceCoverage, passed: signals.sliceCoverage >= thresholds.sliceCoverage, role: 'blocking_guard' },
264:     { signal: 'reuseCatalogCoverage', value: signals.reuseCatalogCoverage, threshold: thresholds.reuseCatalogCoverage, passed: signals.reuseCatalogCoverage >= thresholds.reuseCatalogCoverage, role: 'weighted' },
265:     { signal: 'agreementRate', value: signals.agreementRate, threshold: thresholds.agreementRate, passed: signals.agreementRate >= thresholds.agreementRate, role: 'blocking_guard' },
266:     { signal: 'relevanceFloor', value: signals.relevanceFloor, threshold: thresholds.relevanceFloor, passed: signals.relevanceFloor >= thresholds.relevanceFloor, role: 'blocking_guard' },
267:     { signal: 'dependencyCompleteness', value: signals.dependencyCompleteness, threshold: thresholds.dependencyCompleteness, passed: signals.dependencyCompleteness >= thresholds.dependencyCompleteness, role: 'weighted' },
268:   ];
269:   if (signals.sliceCoverage < thresholds.sliceCoverage) {
270:     blockers.push({ type: 'uncovered_slices', description: `Slice coverage (${(signals.sliceCoverage * 100).toFixed(0)}%) is below threshold (${(thresholds.sliceCoverage * 100).toFixed(0)}%). ${gaps.length} gap(s) found. STOP is blocked until the in-scope surface is swept.`, count: gaps.length, severity: 'blocking' });
271:   }
272:   if (signals.relevanceFloor < thresholds.relevanceFloor) {
273:     blockers.push({ type: 'low_relevance_focus', description: `Relevance floor (${(signals.relevanceFloor * 100).toFixed(0)}%) is below threshold. The loop is collecting mostly low-relevance context; STOP is blocked until findings clear the relevance gate.`, count: 1, severity: 'blocking' });
274:   }
275:   if (signals.agreementRate < thresholds.agreementRate) {
276:     blockers.push({ type: 'low_cross_executor_agreement', description: `Cross-executor agreement (${(signals.agreementRate * 100).toFixed(0)}%) is below threshold. Findings are not yet confirmed by multiple model lenses; STOP is blocked.`, count: 1, severity: 'blocking' });
277:   }
278:   if (contradictions.length > 0) {
279:     blockers.push({ type: 'context_contradictions', description: `${contradictions.length} contradiction(s) detected across executors; reconcile before STOP.`, count: contradictions.length, severity: 'warning' });
280:   }
281:   return { blockers, trace };
282: }
283: 
284: function decisionReason(decision, blockingBlockers, trace) {
285:   if (decision === 'STOP_ALLOWED') return 'All convergence signals pass thresholds; STOP is allowed pending newInfoRatio agreement.';
286:   if (decision === 'STOP_BLOCKED') {
287:     return `STOP is blocked by ${blockingBlockers.length} blocker(s): ${blockingBlockers.map((blocker) => blocker.type).join(', ')}`;
288:   }
289:   return `Convergence signals not yet met: ${trace.filter((entry) => !entry.passed).map((entry) => entry.signal).join(', ')}. Continue iterating.`;
290: }
291: 
292: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
293: // 5. CORE LOGIC
294: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
295: 
296: async function main() {
297:   const args = parseArgs();
298:   const specFolder = validateNamespaceValue(ensureString(args, 'specFolder'), 'specFolder', inputError);
299:   const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
300:   const sessionId = validateNamespaceValue(ensureString(args, 'sessionId'), 'sessionId', inputError);
301:   if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review' && loopType !== 'council' && loopType !== 'context') {
302:     throw inputError('loopType must be "research", "review", "council", or "context"');
303:   }
304: 
305:   const ns = { specFolder, loopType, sessionId };
306:   let db = null;
307: 
308:   try {
309:     const isCouncil = loopType === 'council';
310:     db = isCouncil
311:       ? await import('../lib/council/council-graph-db.ts')
312:       : await import('../lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts');
313:     installSignalHandlers(() => db?.closeDb());
314:     maybeThrowTestFault();
315:     if (isCouncil) {
316:       if (asBoolean(args.persistSnapshot) && !args.roundId) {
317:         throw inputError('--round-id is required when --persist-snapshot is true for council convergence');
318:       }
319:       const councilConvergence = require('../lib/council/convergence.cjs');
320:       const data = await councilConvergence.evaluateCouncilConvergence(ns, {
321:         roundId: args.roundId,
322:         persistSnapshot: asBoolean(args.persistSnapshot),
323:       });
324:       jsonOut(councilConvergence.bridgePayload(data));
325:       return;
326:     }
327: 
328:     const signalsLib = await import('../lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts');
329:     const queryLib = await import('../lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts');
330:     const nodes = db.getNodes(ns);
331:     const edges = db.getEdges(ns);
332:     const snapshots = db.getSnapshots(specFolder, loopType, sessionId);
333:     const stats = {
334:       totalNodes: nodes.length,
335:       totalEdges: edges.length,
336:       nodesByKind: nodes.reduce((acc, node) => ({ ...acc, [node.kind]: (acc[node.kind] || 0) + 1 }), {}),
337:       edgesByRelation: edges.reduce((acc, edge) => ({ ...acc, [edge.relation]: (acc[edge.relation] || 0) + 1 }), {}),
338:       lastIteration: snapshots.length > 0 ? snapshots[snapshots.length - 1].iteration : null,
339:     };
340: 
341:     if (stats.totalNodes === 0) {
342:       const data = {
343:         decision: 'CONTINUE',
344:         reason: 'Graph is empty; insufficient data for convergence assessment',
345:         signals: null,
346:         blockers: [],
347:         trace: [],
348:         namespace: ns,
349:         scopeMode: 'session',
350:         nodeCount: 0,
351:         edgeCount: 0,
352:       };
353:       jsonOut({ status: 'ok', data, graph_decision: data.decision, graph_decision_json: JSON.stringify(data.decision), graph_signals_json: {}, graph_blockers_json: [], graph_blockers_csv: '', graph_stop_blocked: false, graph_trace_json: [], graph_convergence_score: 0 });
354:       return;
355:     }
356: 
357:     const signals = loopType === 'research'
358:       ? {
359:           questionCoverage: signalsLib.computeResearchQuestionCoverageFromData(nodes, edges),
360:           claimVerificationRate: signalsLib.computeResearchClaimVerificationRateFromData(nodes),
361:           contradictionDensity: signalsLib.computeResearchContradictionDensityFromData(edges),
362:           sourceDiversity: signalsLib.computeResearchSourceDiversityFromData(nodes, edges),
363:           evidenceDepth: signalsLib.computeResearchEvidenceDepthFromData(nodes, edges),
364:         }
365:       : loopType === 'context'
366:         ? signalsLib.computeContextSignalsFromData(nodes, edges)
367:         : buildReviewSignals(nodes, edges);
368:     const score = computeCompositeScore(signals, loopType);
369:     const signalsWithScore = { ...signals, score };
370:     const gaps = queryLib.findCoverageGaps(ns);
371:     const contradictions = queryLib.findContradictions(ns);
372:     const unverified = loopType === 'research' ? queryLib.findUnverifiedClaims(ns) : [];
373:     const evaluated = loopType === 'research'
374:       ? evaluateResearch(signals, gaps, contradictions, unverified)
375:       : loopType === 'context'
376:         ? evaluateContext(signals, gaps, contradictions)
377:         : evaluateReview(signals, gaps, contradictions);
378:     const blockingBlockers = evaluated.blockers.filter((blocker) => blocker.severity === 'blocking');
379:     const decision = blockingBlockers.length > 0
380:       ? 'STOP_BLOCKED'
381:       : evaluated.trace.every((entry) => entry.passed) ? 'STOP_ALLOWED' : 'CONTINUE';
382:     const momentum = snapshots.length < 2
383:       ? null
384:       : Object.fromEntries(Object.keys(snapshots[snapshots.length - 1].metrics || {}).flatMap((key) => {
385:           const latest = snapshots[snapshots.length - 1].metrics[key];
386:           const previous = snapshots[snapshots.length - 2].metrics[key];
387:           return typeof latest === 'number' && typeof previous === 'number' ? [[key, latest - previous]] : [];
388:         }));
389: 
390:     if (asBoolean(args.persistSnapshot) && args.iteration !== undefined) {
391:       // Snapshot writes share the deep-loop graph DB with upsert.cjs, so they
392:       // must take the same writer lock to avoid a concurrent-write race.
393:       const releaseWriterLock = acquireWriterLock(path.join(db.COVERAGE_GRAPH_DATABASE_DIR, '.deep-loop-graph-writer.lock'));
394:       try {
395:         db.createSnapshot({
396:           specFolder,
397:           loopType,
398:           sessionId,
399:           iteration: Number(args.iteration),
400:           metrics: { ...signals, nodeCount: stats.totalNodes, edgeCount: stats.totalEdges },
401:           nodeCount: stats.totalNodes,
402:           edgeCount: stats.totalEdges,
403:         });
404:       } finally {
405:         releaseWriterLock();
406:       }
407:     }
408: 
409:     const data = {
410:       decision,
411:       reason: decisionReason(decision, blockingBlockers, evaluated.trace),
412:       score,
413:       signals: signalsWithScore,
414:       blockers: evaluated.blockers,
415:       trace: evaluated.trace,
416:       momentum,
417:       namespace: ns,
418:       scopeMode: 'session',
419:       notes: ['Convergence signals were computed from the session-scoped subgraph only.'],
420:       snapshotPersistence: asBoolean(args.persistSnapshot) ? 'persisted' : 'not_requested',
421:       nodeCount: stats.totalNodes,
422:       edgeCount: stats.totalEdges,
423:       lastIteration: stats.lastIteration,
424:     };
425:     jsonOut({
426:       status: 'ok',
427:       data,
428:       graph_decision: decision,
429:       graph_decision_json: JSON.stringify(decision),
430:       graph_signals_json: signalsWithScore,
431:       graph_blockers_json: evaluated.blockers,
432:       graph_blockers_csv: blockersCsv(evaluated.blockers),
433:       graph_stop_blocked: decision === 'STOP_BLOCKED',
434:       graph_trace_json: evaluated.trace,
435:       graph_convergence_score: score,
436:     });
437:   } finally {

2: //
3: // Runtime-owned SQLite projection for AI Council graph state. This is a
4: // derived index: packet-local ai-council artifacts remain source-of-truth.

9: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
10: 
11: // ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
12: 
13: export type CouncilNodeKind =

15:   | 'ROUND'
16:   | 'SEAT'
17:   | 'CLAIM'
18:   | 'EVIDENCE'
19:   | 'DISAGREEMENT'
20:   | 'DECISION'
21:   | 'RECOMMENDATION';
22: 
23: export type CouncilRelation =
24:   | 'PARTICIPATES_IN'
25:   | 'PROPOSES'
26:   | 'SUPPORTS'
27:   | 'CONTRADICTS'
28:   | 'DERIVES_FROM'
29:   | 'AGREES_WITH'
30:   | 'RESOLVES'
31:   | 'ESCALATES'
32:   | 'EVIDENCE_FOR'
33:   | 'RECOMMENDS';
34: 
35: export interface CouncilNode {
36:   id: string;
37:   specFolder: string;
38:   sessionId: string;
39:   kind: CouncilNodeKind;
40:   name: string;
41:   artifactPath?: string;
42:   contentHash?: string;
43:   roundId?: string;
44:   metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
45:   createdAt?: string;
46:   updatedAt?: string;
47: }
48: 
49: export interface CouncilEdge {

51:   specFolder: string;
52:   sessionId: string;
53:   sourceId: string;
54:   targetId: string;
55:   relation: CouncilRelation;
56:   weight: number;
57:   artifactPath?: string;

62: export interface CouncilSnapshot {

73: export interface CouncilNamespace {

75:   sessionId?: string;
76: }

78: // ───── CONSTANTS ─────

80: export const SCHEMA_VERSION = 1;
81: export const DB_FILENAME = 'council-graph.sqlite';
82: export const COUNCIL_GRAPH_STORAGE_DIR = join(
83:   dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)),
84:   '..',
85:   '..',
86:   'database',
87: );

89: const MIN_WEIGHT = 0.0;
90: const MAX_WEIGHT = 2.0;
91: 
92: export const VALID_KINDS = [
93:   'SESSION',
94:   'ROUND',
95:   'SEAT',
96:   'CLAIM',
97:   'EVIDENCE',
98:   'DISAGREEMENT',
99:   'DECISION',
100:   'RECOMMENDATION',
101: ] as const;
102: 
103: export const VALID_RELATIONS = [
104:   'PARTICIPATES_IN',
105:   'PROPOSES',
106:   'SUPPORTS',
107:   'CONTRADICTS',
108:   'DERIVES_FROM',
109:   'AGREES_WITH',
110:   'RESOLVES',
111:   'ESCALATES',
112:   'EVIDENCE_FOR',
113:   'RECOMMENDS',
114: ] as const;
115: 
116: const KIND_CHECK = VALID_KINDS.map((kind) => `'${kind}'`).join(', ');

30: function findRepoRoot(startDir) {

44:   return opencodeMatch || path.resolve(startDir, '..', '..', '..', '..', '..');

49: function hasTraversalSegment(value) {

53: function isInsideRepo(resolvedPath) {

226:   let ageMs = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY;
227:   if (Number.isFinite(tsFromBody)) {

229:   } else {
230:     // No usable timestamp in the body (half-written or legacy) — fall back to

232:     try {

234:     } catch {
235:       ageMs = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY;

239:   // A live holder inside the hold window owns the lock; respect it.
240:   if (Number.isInteger(pid) && pid > 0 && isPidAlive(pid) && ageMs < maxHoldMs) {
241:     return false;
242:   }
243:   // Dead pid, or any lock older than the hold window, is reclaimable. A lock
244:   // with no parseable pid is stale only after it has aged past the window.
245:   return ageMs >= maxHoldMs || (Number.isInteger(pid) && pid > 0 && !isPidAlive(pid));
246: }
247: 
248: /**
249:  * Read and parse the lock file body, returning the stored nonce (or null).
250:  * Defensive on every failure path: a missing, empty, half-written, or non-JSON
251:  * file — and a body without a string `nonce` — all yield null, which callers
252:  * treat as "not mine". This is the ownership check both the P0 acquire re-read
253:  * and the P1 release use, so a lost race never deletes a peer's valid lock.
254:  *
255:  * @param {string} lockPath - Path to the lock file.
256:  * @returns {string|null} The stored nonce when present and well-formed, else null.
257:  */
258: function readLockNonce(lockPath) {
259:   let raw;
260:   try {
261:     raw = fs.readFileSync(lockPath, 'utf8');
262:   } catch {
263:     return null;
264:   }
265:   let parsed = null;
266:   try {
267:     const trimmed = typeof raw === 'string' ? raw.trim() : '';
268:     if (trimmed) parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed);
269:   } catch {
270:     return null;
271:   }
272:   if (parsed && typeof parsed === 'object' && typeof parsed.nonce === 'string' && parsed.nonce) {
273:     return parsed.nonce;
274:   }
275:   return null;
276: }
277: 
278: /**
279:  * Acquire an exclusive writer lock via `wx` file open on the given path.
280:  * Returns a release function that closes the file descriptor and removes
281:  * the lock file. Retries briefly on contention so parallel test files and
282:  * near-simultaneous runtime callers do not fail on transient writer overlap.
283:  *
284:  * Self-heals orphaned locks: the owner pid, a creation timestamp, and a unique
285:  * per-acquisition nonce are written into the lock file. On EEXIST a lock held by
286:  * a dead pid (or one older than the max-hold age) is reclaimed (best-effort
287:  * remove, then retry). A lock held by a LIVE pid inside the age window keeps the
288:  * original maxWait/retry-deadline-then-throw behavior.
289:  *
290:  * The nonce closes two mutual-exclusion races against a concurrent reclaimer:
291:  *  - Acquire: after `wx` + stamp, the file is re-read; if it no longer carries
292:  *    THIS acquisition's nonce (a peer rmSync'd ours and recreated theirs in the
293:  *    window), the fd is closed and the acquire loop re-evaluated. The lock is
294:  *    only considered held once the on-disk body carries our nonce.
295:  *  - Release: the lock is removed only if its on-disk body still carries our
296:  *    nonce. If we were reclaimed mid-write, release skips the rmSync (but still
297:  *    closes the fd) so it never deletes the new holder's lock.
298:  *
299:  * @param {string} lockPath - Path to the lock file to create.
300:  * @returns {Function} Release function (call to unlock and clean up).
301:  * @throws {Error} With code DB_ERROR if the lock remains held after retry.
302:  */

304:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(lockPath), { recursive: true });
305:   const maxWaitMs = readNonNegativeEnvNumber('DEEP_LOOP_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS', DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS);
306:   const retryIntervalMs = readNonNegativeEnvNumber('DEEP_LOOP_WRITER_LOCK_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS', DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS);
307:   const maxHoldMs = readNonNegativeEnvNumber('DEEP_LOOP_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_HOLD_MS', DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_HOLD_MS);
308:   const deadline = Date.now() + maxWaitMs;
309:   // Cap reclaim attempts so a never-removable or re-stamped stale lock falls
310:   // through to the maxWait/throw path instead of spinning indefinitely.
311:   const MAX_RECLAIM_ATTEMPTS = 8;
312:   let reclaimAttempts = 0;
313:   // Unique per acquisition. Stamped into the lock body so a concurrent reclaimer
314:   // that rmSync's our file and recreates its own is detectable: the on-disk
315:   // nonce will no longer be ours. Drives both the acquire re-read and release.
316:   const nonce = randomUUID();
317:   let fd;
318: 
319:   while (true) {
320:     try {
321:       fd = fs.openSync(lockPath, 'wx');
322:       // Stamp owner pid + creation time + nonce so other acquirers can detect a
323:       // stale (dead-pid or aged-out) lock and so we can prove ownership. A write
324:       // failure must not forfeit a lock we legitimately hold — staleness then
325:       // falls back to mtime (still fresh). But without a stamped nonce we cannot
326:       // distinguish our own lock from a peer's at re-read/release time, so on a
327:       // stamp failure fall through to the contention path and retry rather than
328:       // claim an unverifiable lock.
329:       let stamped = false;
330:       try {
331:         fs.writeSync(fd, JSON.stringify({ pid: process.pid, ts: Date.now(), nonce }));
332:         stamped = true;
333:       } catch {
334:         // Could not stamp; treat as not-acquired and recover below.
335:       }
336:       if (!stamped) {
337:         // Release the half-formed lock so a peer (or our own retry) can take it,
338:         // then loop to re-acquire cleanly.
339:         try {
340:           fs.closeSync(fd);
341:         } catch {
342:           // best-effort
343:         }
344:         fd = undefined;
345:         try {
346:           // Only remove if it is the file we just (unstamped) created. Empty
347:           // body → readLockNonce returns null → not ours, so skip; otherwise a
348:           // peer beat us between open and here.
349:           if (readLockNonce(lockPath) === null) fs.rmSync(lockPath, { force: true });
350:         } catch {
351:           // best-effort
352:         }
353:         const remainingMs = deadline - Date.now();
354:         if (maxWaitMs <= 0 || remainingMs <= 0) {
355:           const lockErr = new Error('deep-loop graph writer lock could not be stamped');
356:           lockErr.code = 'DB_ERROR';
357:           throw lockErr;
358:         }
359:         sleepSync(Math.min(retryIntervalMs, remainingMs));
360:         continue;
361:       }
362:       // P0 close: re-read the file we just stamped. If a peer reclaimed (rmSync)
363:       // our lock and recreated its own in the window between our openSync and
364:       // this read, the on-disk nonce will not be ours — we are NOT the holder.
365:       // Close our (now unlinked or foreign) fd and loop to re-evaluate, rather
366:       // than entering the critical section on a lock someone else owns.
367:       if (readLockNonce(lockPath) !== nonce) {
368:         try {
369:           fs.closeSync(fd);
370:         } catch {
371:           // best-effort
372:         }
373:         fd = undefined;
374:         const remainingMs = deadline - Date.now();
375:         if (maxWaitMs <= 0 || remainingMs <= 0) {
376:           const lockErr = new Error('deep-loop graph writer lock is held by another process');
377:           lockErr.code = 'DB_ERROR';
378:           throw lockErr;
379:         }
380:         sleepSync(Math.min(retryIntervalMs, remainingMs));
381:         continue;
382:       }
383:       break;
384:     } catch (err) {
385:       const code = err && typeof err === 'object' && 'code' in err ? err.code : undefined;
386:       if (code !== 'EEXIST') throw err;
387: 
388:       // Stale-lock reclamation: if the holder is dead or the lock has aged out,
389:       // remove it and retry the open. This can itself race a second reclaimer,
390:       // so loop — on a re-thrown EEXIST we re-evaluate staleness next pass.
391:       // Bounded by a reclaim-attempt cap so a pathological never-removable lock
392:       // (or a peer that keeps re-stamping a stale ts) cannot spin forever.
393:       if (reclaimAttempts < MAX_RECLAIM_ATTEMPTS && isWriterLockStale(lockPath, maxHoldMs)) {
394:         reclaimAttempts += 1;
395:         try {
396:           fs.rmSync(lockPath, { force: true });
397:         } catch {
398:           // Another reclaimer may have removed it first; the retry settles it.
399:         }
400:         continue;
401:       }
402: 
403:       const remainingMs = deadline - Date.now();
404:       if (maxWaitMs <= 0 || remainingMs <= 0) {
405:         const lockErr = new Error('deep-loop graph writer lock is held by another process');
406:         lockErr.code = 'DB_ERROR';
407:         throw lockErr;
408:       }
409:       sleepSync(Math.min(retryIntervalMs, remainingMs));
410:     }
411:   }
412: 
413:   return () => {
414:     if (typeof fd === 'number') {
415:       try {
416:         fs.closeSync(fd);
417:       } catch {
418:         // Best-effort cleanup during process teardown.
419:       }
420:       fd = undefined;
421:     }
422:     // P1 close: only remove the lock if its on-disk body still carries OUR
423:     // nonce. If we were reclaimed while holding the critical section (our write
424:     // aged past maxHoldMs, or the P0 race), the file now belongs to a new
425:     // holder — deleting it would break their mutual exclusion. A missing,
426:     // half-written, or foreign-nonce file all read as "not mine" → skip rmSync.
427:     try {
428:       if (readLockNonce(lockPath) === nonce) {
429:         fs.rmSync(lockPath, { force: true });
430:       }
431:     } catch {
432:       // Best-effort: never throw from release/teardown.
433:     }
434:   };
435: }
436: 
437: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
438: // 5. EXPORTS
439: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
440: 
441: module.exports = {
442:   acquireWriterLock,
443:   classifyExitCode,
444:   installSignalHandlers,
445:   maybeThrowTestFault,
446:   sleepSync,
447:   validateNamespaceValue,
448: };

(End of file - total 448 lines)
</content>

1: import { afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
2: 
3: import { spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';
4: import { existsSync, mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
5: import { tmpdir } from 'node:os';
6: import { join } from 'node:path';
7: 
8: // cli-guards is CommonJS; pull the writer-lock primitive under test.
9: import { acquireWriterLock } from '../../scripts/lib/cli-guards.cjs';
10: 
11: const MAX_WAIT_ENV = 'DEEP_LOOP_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS';
12: const MAX_HOLD_ENV = 'DEEP_LOOP_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_HOLD_MS';
13: 
14: const tempDirs: string[] = [];
15: 
16: /**
17:  * Creates a fresh temp lock path (unique dir) and tracks it for cleanup.
18:  */
19: function newLockPath(): string {
20:   const dir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'cli-guards-lock-'));
21:   tempDirs.push(dir);
22:   return join(dir, '.deep-loop-graph-writer.lock');
23: }
24: 
25: /**
26:  * Returns a pid that is guaranteed dead: spawnSync runs a no-op child to
27:  * completion, so its pid refers to an already-terminated process.
28:  */
29: function deadPid(): number {
30:   const child = spawnSync(process.execPath, ['-e', 'process.exit(0)']);
31:   return child.pid as number;
32: }
33: 
34: afterEach(() => {
35:   delete process.env[MAX_WAIT_ENV];
36:   delete process.env[MAX_HOLD_ENV];
37:   while (tempDirs.length > 0) {
38:     const dir = tempDirs.pop();
39:     if (dir) rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true });
40:   }
41: });
42: 
43: describe('acquireWriterLock stale-lock reclamation', () => {
44:   it('reclaims a lock owned by a dead pid and acquires successfully', () => {
45:     const lockPath = newLockPath();
46:     const dpid = deadPid();
47:     // Sanity: the pid is truly gone.
48:     expect(() => process.kill(dpid, 0)).toThrow();
49: 
50:     // Fresh ts so reclamation must rely on dead-pid detection, not age-out.
51:     writeFileSync(lockPath, JSON.stringify({ pid: dpid, ts: Date.now() }));
52: 
53:     // maxWait=0 → a wrongly-respected live lock would throw immediately, so a
54:     // successful acquire proves the dead lock was reclaimed (not just waited out).
55:     process.env[MAX_WAIT_ENV] = '0';
56:     const release = acquireWriterLock(lockPath);
57:     expect(typeof release).toBe('function');
58: 
59:     // The reclaimed lock now belongs to this process.
60:     const body = JSON.parse(readFileSync(lockPath, 'utf8'));
61:     expect(Number(body.pid)).toBe(process.pid);
62: 
63:     release();
64:     expect(existsSync(lockPath)).toBe(false);
65:   });
66: 
67:   it('respects a lock held by the current live pid within the age window', () => {
68:     const lockPath = newLockPath();
69:     // Owner = this (live) process, ts = now → inside the max-hold window.
70:     writeFileSync(lockPath, JSON.stringify({ pid: process.pid, ts: Date.now() }));
71: 
72:     // maxWait=0 → fail fast rather than loop to a deadline.
73:     process.env[MAX_WAIT_ENV] = '0';
74:     expect(() => acquireWriterLock(lockPath)).toThrowError(
75:       expect.objectContaining({ code: 'DB_ERROR' }),
76:     );
77: 
78:     // A failed acquirer must not delete a live holder's lock.
79:     expect(existsSync(lockPath)).toBe(true);
80:   });
81: 
82:   it('reclaims an aged-out lock even when the owning pid is still alive', () => {
83:     const lockPath = newLockPath();
84:     // Live owner (us) but ts older than the hold window → reclaimable.
85:     writeFileSync(lockPath, JSON.stringify({ pid: process.pid, ts: Date.now() - 10 * 60_000 }));
86: 
87:     process.env[MAX_WAIT_ENV] = '0';
88:     process.env[MAX_HOLD_ENV] = '60000';
89:     const release = acquireWriterLock(lockPath);
90:     expect(typeof release).toBe('function');
91:     release();
92:     expect(existsSync(lockPath)).toBe(false);
93:   });
94: 
95:   it('round-trips a normal uncontended acquire and release', () => {
96:     const lockPath = newLockPath();
97:     const release = acquireWriterLock(lockPath);
98:     expect(existsSync(lockPath)).toBe(true);
99:     // Lock body is stamped with owner pid + a timestamp + a unique nonce.
100:     const body = JSON.parse(readFileSync(lockPath, 'utf8'));
101:     expect(Number(body.pid)).toBe(process.pid);
102:     expect(typeof body.ts).toBe('number');
103:     expect(typeof body.nonce).toBe('string');
104:     expect(body.nonce.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
105:     release();
106:     expect(existsSync(lockPath)).toBe(false);
107:   });
108: 
109:   it('release does NOT delete a lock it no longer owns', () => {
110:     const lockPath = newLockPath();
111:     // Acquire normally → on-disk body carries our nonce.
112:     const release = acquireWriterLock(lockPath);
113:     expect(existsSync(lockPath)).toBe(true);
114: 
115:     // Simulate being reclaimed mid-critical-section: a peer rmSync'd our lock
116:     // and recreated its own with a DIFFERENT nonce while we still hold our fd.
117:     const foreign = JSON.stringify({ pid: process.pid, ts: Date.now(), nonce: 'foreign-holder-nonce' });
118:     writeFileSync(lockPath, foreign);
119: 
120:     // Release must be ownership-blind no longer: it sees a foreign nonce and
121:     // skips the rmSync, leaving the new holder's lock intact.
122:     release();
123:     expect(existsSync(lockPath)).toBe(true);
124:     const after = JSON.parse(readFileSync(lockPath, 'utf8'));
125:     expect(after.nonce).toBe('foreign-holder-nonce');
126:   });
127: 
128:   it('release skips rmSync on a missing or half-written lock file', () => {
129:     const lockPath = newLockPath();
130:     const release = acquireWriterLock(lockPath);
131: 
132:     // Half-written / non-JSON body reads as "not mine" → release must not throw
133:     // and must not delete it (a peer may be mid-write between open and stamp).
134:     writeFileSync(lockPath, '{ partial');
135:     expect(() => release()).not.toThrow();
136:     expect(existsSync(lockPath)).toBe(true);
137:   });
138: });

(End of file - total 138 lines)
</content>

72: function seedContinuingCouncil(namespace: ScriptNamespace) {
73:   return runScript('upsert', [
74:     ...namespaceArgs(namespace),
75:     '--nodes',
76:     JSON.stringify([
77:       { id: 'claim-1', kind: 'CLAIM', name: 'Graph is useful' },
78:       { id: 'decision-1', kind: 'DECISION', name: 'Continue council graph', metadata: { confidence: 0.82 } },
79:     ]),
80:     '--edges',
81:     JSON.stringify([
82:       { id: 'support-1', sourceId: 'claim-1', targetId: 'decision-1', relation: 'SUPPORTS' },
83:     ]),
84:   ]);
85: }
86: 
87: describe('council graph direct script invocation', () => {
88:   it('treats empty council upsert as explicit no-op success', () => {
89:     const namespace = councilNamespace('upsert');
90:     const result = runScript('upsert', namespaceArgs(namespace));
91:

94:     expect(result.json.data).toMatchObject({
95:       insertedNodes: 0,
96:       insertedEdges: 0,
97:       rejectedEdges: 0,
98:       rejectedSelfLoops: [],
99:       noOp: true,
100:       namespace,
101:       sourceOfTruth: 'derived_from_ai_council_artifacts',
102:     });
103:     expect(result.json.graph_upsert_event_count).toBe(0);
104:   });
105: 
106:   it('upserts council nodes and edges with artifact provenance fields', () => {

108:     const result = seedBlockingCouncil(namespace);
109: 
110:     expect(result.exitCode).toBe(0);
111:     expect(result.json.status).toBe('ok');
112:     expect(result.json.data).toMatchObject({
113:       insertedNodes: 6,
114:       insertedEdges: 4,
115:       rejectedSelfLoops: ['edge-self'],
116:       namespace,
117:       sourceOfTruth: 'derived_from_ai_council_artifacts',
118:     });
119:     expect(result.json.graph_nodes_json).toEqual(
120:       expect.arrayContaining([
121:         expect.objectContaining({
122:           id: 'session',
123:           kind: 'SESSION',
124:           artifactPath: 'ai-council/ai-council-state.jsonl',
125:         }),
126:         expect.objectContaining({ id: 'seat-a', roundId: 'round-001' }),
127:       ]),
128:     );
129:   });
130: 
131:   it('queries each council query type', () => {
132:     const namespace = councilNamespace('query');
133:     expect(seedBlockingCouncil(namespace).exitCode).toBe(0);
134: 
135:     const unresolved = runScript('query', [...namespaceArgs(namespace), '--query-type', 'unresolved_disagreements']);
136:     expect(unresolved.exitCode).toBe(0);
137:     expect(unresolved.json.data).toMatchObject({
138:       totalUnresolved: 1,
139:       sourceOfTruth: 'derived_from_ai_council_artifacts',
140:     });
141:

143:     expect(support.exitCode).toBe(0);
144:     expect((support.json.data as { support: Array<{ supportCount: number; evidenceCount: number }> }).support[0]).toMatchObject({
145:       supportCount: 1,
146:       evidenceCount: 1,
147:     });
148: 
149:     const chain = runScript('query', [...namespaceArgs(namespace), '--query-type', 'evidence_chain', '--node-id', 'decision-1']);
150:     expect(chain.exitCode).toBe(0);
151:     expect((chain.json.data as { chain: Array<{ node: { id: string } }> }).chain.map((step) => step.node.id)).toEqual(
152:       expect.arrayContaining(['decision-1', 'claim-1', 'evidence-1']),
153:     );
154:

156:     expect(blockers.exitCode).toBe(0);

162:     expect(hotNodes.exitCode).toBe(0);

10: # Deep Loop Workflows
11: 
12: One skill, five workflow modes, one shared backend. `deep-loop-workflows` is the public, advisor-routable home for every deep-loop persona; `deep-loop-runtime` is the frozen, MCP-free backend it consumes. This hub holds NO per-mode convergence, state, or synthesis logic — each mode keeps its own contract in its packet, and the hub only routes by `workflowMode` through `mode-registry.json`.
13: 
14: ---
15: 
16: ## 1. WHEN TO USE
17: 
18: Use this skill (through its `/deep:*` commands and native agents) for any deep-loop workflow:
19: 
20: | Mode | Use it for | Command | Agent |
21: |------|-----------|---------|-------|
22: | **context** | Inward codebase-context gathering → reuse-first Context Report, before `/speckit:plan` | `/deep:context` | `deep-context` |

25: | **ai-council** | Multi-seat planning deliberation → `ai-council/**` artifacts | `/deep:ai-council` | `ai-council` |
26: | **improvement** (4 lanes) | Evaluator-first improvement: `agent-improvement`, `model-benchmark`, `skill-benchmark`, `non-dev-ai-system-refine` | `/deep:agent-improvement` · `/deep:model-benchmark` · `/deep:skill-benchmark` · `/deep:ai-system-improvement` | `deep-improvement` |
27: 
28: ### When NOT to Use
29: - A single quick read/edit (no loop) — use the relevant code or doc skill directly.
30: - Backend/runtime support (executor, coverage-graph, scoring, fan-out) — that is `deep-loop-runtime`, consumed here, not invoked as a user workflow.
31: 
32: ---
33: 
34: ## 2. SMART ROUTING
35: 
36: Routing is **registry-driven**. `mode-registry.json` is the single source of truth; the hub, commands, and advisor all read it and none re-derive the mapping.
37: 
38: ### The three-tier discriminator
39: - **`workflowMode`** — the public mode key (all modes): `context`, `research`, `review`, `ai-council`, and the four improvement lanes `agent-improvement`, `model-benchmark`, `skill-benchmark`, `ai-system-improvement` (its loop-host mode stays `non-dev-ai-system-refine`).
40: - **`runtimeLoopType`** — the graph-backed convergence key consumed by `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs` (validated against exactly `research|review|council|context`). **Explicit `null` for all four improvement lanes; never inferred from `workflowMode`.** Note `ai-council` maps to `runtimeLoopType: council`.
41: - **`backendKind`** — which backend runs the mode: `runtime-loop-type` (context/research/review/ai-council), `improvement-host` (`deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs --mode`), or `external-adapter` (non-dev-ai-system; the loop is owned by external packaging).
42: 
43: ### Routing rule
44: ```
45: read mode-registry.json
46:   → resolve workflowMode from the command / advisor alias
47:   → load the mode packet at registry[mode].packet/   (the 4 improvement modes all share the deep-improvement/ packet)
48:   → if registry[mode].runtimeLoopType !== null: backend = convergence.cjs --loop-type <runtimeLoopType>
49:      else: backend = improvement loop-host (--mode) or external adapter, per backendKind
50: ```
51: 
52: Per-mode behavior is **not flattened**: each packet keeps its own convergence math, state shape, artifacts, and tool-permission guards (research has WebFetch; review/context/ai-council are code/inward-only; improvement is the only mutating family).
53: 
54: ---
55: 
56: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS
57: 
58: ### Layout
59: ```
60: deep-loop-workflows/
61:   SKILL.md               # this routing hub (no per-mode logic)
62:   mode-registry.json     # the three-tier discriminator (single source of truth)
63:   graph-metadata.json    # the ONE advisor identity for the whole skill
64:   deep-context/   deep-research/   deep-review/   ai-council/   deep-improvement/   # five verbatim mode packets
65:   shared/synthesis/      # workflows-shared synthesis (e.g. emitResourceMap)
66: ```
67: 
68: Each mode packet is the former skill's content moved verbatim (its own `SKILL.md`, `references/`, `scripts/`, `assets/`, `feature_catalog/`, `manual_testing_playbook/`), with internal paths repointed and **no per-packet `graph-metadata.json`** — only this hub carries one, so the advisor discovers exactly one skill.
69: 
70: ### Backend
71: All modes consume `deep-loop-runtime` (frozen, MCP-free): executor config, prompt-pack, validation, atomic state, coverage-graph, Bayesian scoring, fan-out, the council primitives, and the promoted plumbing (capability resolver, artifact-root, loop-lock CLI, lifecycle taxonomy). The runtime never gains an `improvement` loopType — improvement stays host-driven.
72: 
73: ---
74: 
75: ## 4. RULES
76: 
77: ### ALWAYS
78: - **ALWAYS** resolve a mode through `mode-registry.json`; never hardcode a router mapping.
79: - **ALWAYS** keep each mode's convergence/state/artifact contract in its packet — the hub stays logic-free.
80: - **ALWAYS** keep exactly one `graph-metadata.json` (this hub's) so the advisor sees one skill.
81: 
82: ### NEVER
83: - **NEVER** add an `improvement` `loopType` to `deep-loop-runtime/convergence.cjs` (improvement is host-driven; `runtimeLoopType` stays `null`).
84: - **NEVER** infer `runtimeLoopType` from `workflowMode` — read it from the registry (explicit `null` is load-bearing).
85: - **NEVER** let a read-only mode (context/research/review/ai-council) reach the improvement mutation scripts (`promote-candidate.cjs`/`rollback-candidate.cjs`).
86: - **NEVER** add a `graph-metadata.json` or a discoverable skill marker inside a mode packet or `shared/`.
87:

# Deep Loop Workflows

One skill, five workflow modes, one shared backend. `deep-loop-workflows` is the public, advisor-routable home for every deep-loop persona; `deep-loop-runtime` is the frozen, MCP-free backend it consumes. This hub holds NO per-mode convergence, state, or synthesis logic — each mode keeps its own contract in its packet, and the hub only routes by `workflowMode` through `mode-registry.json`.

---

## 1. WHEN TO USE

Use this skill (through its `/deep:*` commands and native agents) for any deep-loop workflow:

| Mode | Use it for | Command | Agent |
|------|-----------|---------|-------|
| **context** | Inward codebase-context gathering → reuse-first Context Report, before `/speckit:plan` | `/deep:context` | `deep-context` |
| **research** | Outward, web + code iterative investigation → `research/research.md` | `/deep:research` | `deep-research` |
| **review** | Iterative code audit → P0/P1/P2 findings + verdict | `/deep:review` | `deep-review` |
| **ai-council** | Multi-seat planning deliberation → `ai-council/**` artifacts | `/deep:ai-council` | `ai-council` |
| **improvement** (4 lanes) | Evaluator-first improvement: `agent-improvement`, `model-benchmark`, `skill-benchmark`, `non-dev-ai-system-refine` | `/deep:agent-improvement` · `/deep:model-benchmark` · `/deep:skill-benchmark` · `/deep:ai-system-improvement` | `deep-improvement` |

### When NOT to Use
- A single quick read/edit (no loop) — use the relevant code or doc skill directly.
- Backend/runtime support (executor, coverage-graph, scoring, fan-out) — that is `deep-loop-runtime`, consumed here, not invoked as a user workflow.

---

## 2. SMART ROUTING

Routing is **registry-driven**. `mode-registry.json` is the single source of truth; the hub, commands, and advisor all read it and none re-derive the mapping.

### The three-tier discriminator
- **`workflowMode`** — the public mode key (all modes): `context`, `research`, `review`, `ai-council`, and the four improvement lanes `agent-improvement`, `model-benchmark`, `skill-benchmark`, `ai-system-improvement` (its loop-host mode stays `non-dev-ai-system-refine`).
- **`runtimeLoopType`** — the graph-backed convergence key consumed by `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs` (validated against exactly `research|review|council|context`). **Explicit `null` for all four improvement lanes; never inferred from `workflowMode`.** Note `ai-council` maps to `runtimeLoopType: council`.
- **`backendKind`** — which backend runs the mode: `runtime-loop-type` (context/research/review/ai-council), `improvement-host` (`deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs --mode`), or `external-adapter` (non-dev-ai-system; the loop is owned by external packaging).

### Routing rule
```
read mode-registry.json
  → resolve workflowMode from the command / advisor alias
  → load the mode packet at registry[mode].packet/   (the 4 improvement modes all share the deep-improvement/ packet)
  → if registry[mode].runtimeLoopType !== null: backend = convergence.cjs --loop-type <runtimeLoopType>
     else: backend = improvement loop-host (--mode) or external adapter, per backendKind
```

Per-mode behavior is **not flattened**: each packet keeps its own convergence math, state shape, artifacts, and tool-permission guards (research has WebFetch; review/context/ai-council are code/inward-only; improvement is the only mutating family).

---

## 3. HOW IT WORKS

### Layout
```
deep-loop-workflows/
  SKILL.md               # this routing hub (no per-mode logic)
  mode-registry.json     # the three-tier discriminator (single source of truth)
  graph-metadata.json    # the ONE advisor identity for the whole skill
  deep-context/   deep-research/   deep-review/   ai-council/   deep-improvement/   # five verbatim mode packets
  shared/synthesis/      # workflows-shared synthesis (e.g. emitResourceMap)
```

Each mode packet is the former skill's content moved verbatim (its own `SKILL.md`, `references/`, `scripts/`, `assets/`, `feature_catalog/`, `manual_testing_playbook/`), with internal paths repointed and **no per-packet `graph-metadata.json`** — only this hub carries one, so the advisor discovers exactly one skill.

### Backend
All modes consume `deep-loop-runtime` (frozen, MCP-free): executor config, prompt-pack, validation, atomic state, coverage-graph, Bayesian scoring, fan-out, the council primitives, and the promoted plumbing (capability resolver, artifact-root, loop-lock CLI, lifecycle taxonomy). The runtime never gains an `improvement` loopType — improvement stays host-driven.

---

## 4. RULES

### ALWAYS
- **ALWAYS** resolve a mode through `mode-registry.json`; never hardcode a router mapping.
- **ALWAYS** keep each mode's convergence/state/artifact contract in its packet — the hub stays logic-free.
- **ALWAYS** keep exactly one `graph-metadata.json` (this hub's) so the advisor sees one skill.

### NEVER
- **NEVER** add an `improvement` `loopType` to `deep-loop-runtime/convergence.cjs` (improvement is host-driven; `runtimeLoopType` stays `null`).
- **NEVER** infer `runtimeLoopType` from `workflowMode` — read it from the registry (explicit `null` is load-bearing).
- **NEVER** let a read-only mode (context/research/review/ai-council) reach the improvement mutation scripts (`promote-candidate.cjs`/`rollback-candidate.cjs`).
- **NEVER** add a `graph-metadata.json` or a discoverable skill marker inside a mode packet or `shared/`.

### ESCALATE IF
- A new mode is needed beyond the eight registered — extend `mode-registry.json` and open a packet, do not bolt logic onto the hub.
- A change would require the runtime to gain MCP tools or an improvement loopType — that contradicts the architecture; escalate.

---

## 5. REFERENCES

- Backend: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/` (frozen, consumed by every mode).
- Mode packets: `deep-context/SKILL.md`, `deep-research/SKILL.md`, `deep-review/SKILL.md`, `ai-council/SKILL.md`, `deep-improvement/SKILL.md` (per-mode detail).
- Commands: the eight `/deep:*` commands under `.opencode/commands/deep/`.
- Registry: `mode-registry.json` (the routing contract).

  Line 37: Several deep loops shared the same runtime code. Each needed executor config parsing. Each needed atomic state-log writes and single-writer locking. Each needed a coverage graph that survives a session crash. Each needed Bayesian convergence scoring. The consumer modes depended on the internals of a separate package, every workflow call paid the MCP marshalling and JSON-parse round-trip, and each loop would otherwise duplicate executor config, atomic-state writes, JSONL repair, single-writer locking, coverage-graph ownership, Bayesian scoring and fallback routing.

The coverage-graph family owns the SQLite schema, query builders and convergence-signal extraction. The council family owns multi-seat dispatch, round-state JSONL, adjudicator-verdict scoring, cost guards, session-state hierarchy and the council graph. The workflow modes import what they need. The core scripts replace the deleted MCP tools: `convergence.cjs` computes the stop decision, `upsert.cjs` stores nodes and edges from an iteration's graph events, `query.cjs` inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims and contradictions, and `status.cjs` returns a session-scoped health report. Each parses argv, opens the SQLite database inside a `try`, calls the matching lib function, writes JSON to stdout, closes the database in a `finally` and exits with the standardized code (0 ok, 1 script error, 2 DB error, 3 input validation error).

179: // ───── DATABASE LIFECYCLE ─────
180: 
181: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
182: let dbPath: string | null = null;
183: let statementDb: Database.Database | null = null;
184: const preparedStatements = new Map<string, Database.Statement>();
185: 
186: /**
187:  * Initialize (or get) the council graph database.
188:  *
189:  * Creates the runtime database directory and file, applies the schema, and
190:  * enables WAL journaling and foreign keys.
191:  *
192:  * @param dbDir - Directory for the database file.
193:  * @returns The initialized Database instance.
194:  */
195: export function initDb(dbDir: string = COUNCIL_GRAPH_STORAGE_DIR): Database.Database {
196:   if (db) return db;
197: 
198:   try {
199:     mkdirSync(dbDir, { recursive: true });
200:     dbPath = join(dbDir, DB_FILENAME);
201:     db = new Database(dbPath);
202:     db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
203:     db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
204:     db.exec(SCHEMA_SQL);
205: 
206:     const versionRow = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM council_schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
207:     if (!versionRow) {
208:       db.prepare('INSERT INTO council_schema_version (version) VALUES (?)').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
209:     } else if (versionRow.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
210:       db.prepare('UPDATE council_schema_version SET version = ?').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
211:     }
212: 
213:     return db;
214:   } catch (err: unknown) {
215:     if (db) {
216:       try { db.close(); } catch { }
217:     }
218:     db = null;
219:     dbPath = null;
220:     statementDb = null;
221:     preparedStatements.clear();
222:     throw err;
223:   }
224: }
225: 
226: /**
227:  * Get the current database instance (lazy-initializes if needed).
228:  *
229:  * @returns The Database instance.
230:  */
231: export function getDb(): Database.Database {
232:   if (!db) initDb(COUNCIL_GRAPH_STORAGE_DIR);
233:   return db!;
234: }
235: 
236: /**
237:  * Close the database connection and clear cached prepared statements.
238:  */
239: export function closeDb(): void {
240:   if (db) {
241:     db.close();
242:     db = null;
243:     dbPath = null;
244:     statementDb = null;
245:     preparedStatements.clear();
246:   }
247: }
248: 
249: /**
250:  * Return the active database file path, if a connection has been opened.
251:  *
252:  * @returns Active SQLite file path or null.
253:  */
254: export function getDbPath(): string | null {
255:   return dbPath;
256: }
257: 
258: // ───── HELPERS ─────
259: 
260: function prepareStatement(sql: string): Database.Statement {
261:   const currentDb = getDb();
262:   if (statementDb !== currentDb) {
263:     preparedStatements.clear();
264:     statementDb = currentDb;
265:   }
266: 
267:   let statement = preparedStatements.get(sql);
268:   if (!statement) {
269:     statement = currentDb.prepare(sql);
270:     preparedStatements.set(sql, statement);
271:   }
272:   return statement;
273: }
274: 
275: function buildNamespaceWhere(ns: CouncilNamespace): { clause: string; params: unknown[] } {
276:   const parts = ['spec_folder = ?'];
277:   const params: unknown[] = [ns.specFolder];
278:   if (ns.sessionId) {
279:     parts.push('session_id = ?');
280:     params.push(ns.sessionId);
281:   }
282:   return { clause: parts.join(' AND '), params };
283: }
284: 
285: function rowToNode(row: Record<string, unknown>): CouncilNode {
286:   return {
287:     id: row.id as string,
288:     specFolder: row.spec_folder as string,
289:     sessionId: row.session_id as string,
290:     kind: row.kind as CouncilNodeKind,
291:     name: row.name as string,
292:     artifactPath: (row.artifact_path as string | null) ?? undefined,
293:     contentHash: (row.content_hash as string | null) ?? undefined,
294:     roundId: (row.round_id as string | null) ?? undefined,
295:     metadata: row.metadata ? JSON.parse(row.metadata as string) as Record<string, unknown> : undefined,
296:     createdAt: row.created_at as string | undefined,
297:     updatedAt: row.updated_at as string | undefined,
298:   };
299: }
300: 
301: function rowToEdge(row: Record<string, unknown>): CouncilEdge {
302:   return {
303:     id: row.id as string,
304:     specFolder: row.spec_folder as string,
305:     sessionId: row.session_id as string,
306:     sourceId: row.source_id as string,
307:     targetId: row.target_id as string,
308:     relation: row.relation as CouncilRelation,
309:     weight: row.weight as number,
310:     artifactPath: (row.artifact_path as string | null) ?? undefined,
311:     metadata: row.metadata ? JSON.parse(row.metadata as string) as Record<string, unknown> : undefined,
312:     createdAt: row.created_at as string | undefined,
313:   };
314: }
315: 
316: function rowToSnapshot(row: Record<string, unknown>): CouncilSnapshot {
317:   const roundId = row.round_id === '__latest__' ? undefined : (row.round_id as string | null) ?? undefined;
318:   return {
319:     id: row.id as number,
320:     specFolder: row.spec_folder as string,
321:     sessionId: row.session_id as string,
322:     roundId,
323:     metrics: row.metrics ? JSON.parse(row.metrics as string) as Record<string, unknown> : {},
324:     nodeCount: row.node_count as number,
325:     edgeCount: row.edge_count as number,
326:     createdAt: row.created_at as string | undefined,
327:   };
328: }

330: // ───── EXPORTS ─────
331: 
332: /**
333:  * Clamp relation weight to the valid range [0.0, 2.0].
334:  *
335:  * @param weight - Raw weight value.
336:  * @returns Clamped weight in [0.0, 2.0]. Non-finite values default to 1.0.
337:  */
338: export function clampWeight(weight: number): number {
339:   if (!Number.isFinite(weight)) return 1.0;
340:   return Math.max(MIN_WEIGHT, Math.min(MAX_WEIGHT, weight));
341: }
342: 
343: /**
344:  * Check whether a string is a valid council node kind.
345:  *
346:  * @param kind - Candidate node kind.
347:  * @returns True when the kind belongs to the council taxonomy.
348:  */
349: export function isCouncilNodeKind(kind: string): kind is CouncilNodeKind {
350:   return (VALID_KINDS as readonly string[]).includes(kind);
351: }
352: 
353: /**
354:  * Check whether a string is a valid council relation.

1: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
2: // ║ Runtime Capabilities Resolver (shared backend)                          ║
3: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
4: // ║ Parameterized capability-matrix resolver shared by every graph-backed    ║
5: // ║ deep-loop mode. The per-skill scripts are thin shims that bind a label   ║
6: // ║ (used in the not-found error) and a default matrix path, then re-export  ║
7: // ║ the same four functions so their CLI and module surface stay identical.  ║
8: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
9: 
10: 'use strict';
11: 
12: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
13: // 1. IMPORTS
14: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
15: 
16: const fs = require('node:fs');
17: const path = require('node:path');
18: 
19: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20: // 2. FACTORY
21: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22: 
23: /**
24:  * Build a capability resolver bound to one skill's label and default matrix path.
25:  *
26:  * The returned functions are behaviorally identical to the historical per-skill
27:  * resolver: `loadRuntimeCapabilities`, `listRuntimeCapabilityIds`, and
28:  * `resolveRuntimeCapability` accept an optional override path and otherwise use
29:  * the bound default. `runMain` reproduces the legacy CLI byte-for-byte.
30:  *
31:  * @param {Object} options
32:  * @param {string} options.label - Skill label used in the not-found error message
33:  *   (e.g. "deep-research"). Keeps error strings per-skill identical to pre-merge.
34:  * @param {string} options.defaultCapabilityPath - Default path to runtime_capabilities.json
35:  *   (the caller computes this from its own __dirname so the resolved path is unchanged).
36:  * @returns {{
37:  *   DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_PATH: string,
38:  *   loadRuntimeCapabilities: Function,
39:  *   listRuntimeCapabilityIds: Function,
40:  *   resolveRuntimeCapability: Function,
41:  *   runMain: Function
42:  * }}
43:  */
44: function createRuntimeCapabilities(options = {}) {
45:   const { label, defaultCapabilityPath: DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_PATH } = options;
46: 
47:   if (typeof label !== 'string' || !label) {
48:     throw new Error('createRuntimeCapabilities: a non-empty "label" is required');
49:   }
50:   if (typeof DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_PATH !== 'string' || !DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_PATH) {
51:     throw new Error('createRuntimeCapabilities: a non-empty "defaultCapabilityPath" is required');
52:   }
53: 
54:   /**
55:    * Load and validate the runtime capability matrix from disk.
56:    *
57:    * @param {string} [capabilityPath] - Path to runtime_capabilities.json
58:    * @returns {{ capabilityPath: string, matrix: Object }} Resolved path and parsed matrix
59:    * @throws {Error} If the matrix file is missing or malformed
60:    */
61:   function loadRuntimeCapabilities(capabilityPath = DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_PATH) {
62:     const resolvedPath = path.resolve(capabilityPath);
63:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(resolvedPath, 'utf8'));
64: 
65:     if (!Array.isArray(parsed.runtimes)) {
66:       throw new Error(`Invalid runtime capability matrix at ${resolvedPath}: missing runtimes array`);
67:     }
68: 
69:     return {
70:       capabilityPath: resolvedPath,
71:       matrix: parsed,
72:     };
73:   }
74: 
75:   /**
76:    * List all known runtime IDs from the capability matrix.
77:    *
78:    * @param {string} [capabilityPath] - Path to runtime_capabilities.json
79:    * @returns {string[]} Array of runtime identifier strings
80:    */
81:   function listRuntimeCapabilityIds(capabilityPath = DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_PATH) {
82:     const { matrix } = loadRuntimeCapabilities(capabilityPath);
83:     return matrix.runtimes.map((runtime) => runtime.id);
84:   }
85: 
86:   /**
87:    * Resolve a single runtime's capability record by ID.
88:    *
89:    * @param {string} runtimeId - Runtime identifier to look up
90:    * @param {string} [capabilityPath] - Path to runtime_capabilities.json
91:    * @returns {{ capabilityPath: string, runtime: Object }} Resolved path and runtime record
92:    * @throws {Error} If the runtime ID is not found in the matrix
93:    */
94:   function resolveRuntimeCapability(runtimeId, capabilityPath = DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_PATH) {
95:     const { capabilityPath: resolvedPath, matrix } = loadRuntimeCapabilities(capabilityPath);
96:     const runtime = matrix.runtimes.find((entry) => entry.id === runtimeId);
97: 
98:     if (!runtime) {
99:       throw new Error(
100:         `Unknown ${label} runtime "${runtimeId}". Known runtimes: ${matrix.runtimes.map((entry) => entry.id).join(', ')}. Matrix: ${resolvedPath}`,
101:       );
102:     }
103: 
104:     return {
105:       capabilityPath: resolvedPath,
106:       runtime,
107:     };
108:   }
109: 
110:   /**
111:    * Reproduce the historical CLI: no argument lists all runtime IDs and exits 0;
112:    * a runtime ID prints its resolved record. Output bytes and exit codes match
113:    * the pre-merge per-skill scripts exactly.
114:    *
115:    * @param {string[]} [argv] - Full process argv (argv[2] is the optional runtime id)
116:    */
117:   function runMain(argv = process.argv) {
118:     const runtimeId = argv[2];
119: 
120:     if (!runtimeId) {
121:       process.stdout.write(
122:         `${JSON.stringify(
123:           {
124:             capabilityPath: path.resolve(DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_PATH),
125:             runtimeIds: listRuntimeCapabilityIds(),
126:           },
127:           null,
128:           2,
129:         )}\n`,
130:       );
131:       process.exit(0);
132:     }
133: 
134:     process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(resolveRuntimeCapability(runtimeId), null, 2)}\n`);
135:   }
136: 
137:   return {
138:     DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_PATH,
139:     loadRuntimeCapabilities,
140:     listRuntimeCapabilityIds,
141:     resolveRuntimeCapability,
142:     runMain,
143:   };
144: }
145: 
146: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
147: // 3. EXPORTS
148: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
149: 
150: module.exports = {
151:   createRuntimeCapabilities,
152: };

89: function buildNamespacePredicate(alias: string, ns: Namespace): SqlFragment {
90:   const prefix = alias ? `${alias}.` : '';
91:   const clauses = [`${prefix}spec_folder = ?`, `${prefix}loop_type = ?`];

111:       AND ${nodeAlias}.loop_type = ${edgeAlias}.loop_type
112:       AND ${nodeAlias}.session_id = ${edgeAlias}.session_id
113:       AND ${nodeAlias}.id = ${edgeAlias}.${edgeNodeColumn}`;
114: }

164:     inDegree.set(node.id, 0);
165:   }
166: 
167:   for (const edge of edges) {
168:     if (!adjacency.has(edge.sourceId)) adjacency.set(edge.sourceId, []);
169:     adjacency.get(edge.sourceId)!.push(edge.targetId);
170:     inDegree.set(edge.targetId, (inDegree.get(edge.targetId) ?? 0) + 1);
171:     if (!inDegree.has(edge.sourceId)) inDegree.set(edge.sourceId, 0);
172:   }

174:   const depthMap = new Map<string, number>();

192:       if (candidateDepth > (depthMap.get(childId) ?? 0)) {
193:         depthMap.set(childId, candidateDepth);
194:       }

590:  * Compute context convergence signals from raw node and edge data.
591:  *
592:  * Pure (no DB) so both computeContextSignals and the convergence script share
593:  * one implementation. Agreement is read from a node's CONFIRMS in-edges OR its
594:  * metadata.confirmations (whichever is higher); relevance from metadata.relevance.
595:  * Each signal vacuous-passes (1.0) when its kind is absent so a feature with no
596:  * dependencies (etc.) is not penalized — matching the review p0ResolutionRate rule.
597:  *
598:  * @param nodes - Coverage nodes for the namespace.
599:  * @param edges - Coverage edges for the namespace.
600:  * @returns Context convergence signals with all five metrics.
601:  */
602: export function computeContextSignalsFromData(
603:   nodes: CoverageNode[],
604:   edges: CoverageEdge[],
605: ): ContextConvergenceSignals {
606:   const sliceNodes = nodes.filter((node) => node.kind === 'SLICE');
607:   const reuseNodes = nodes.filter((node) => node.kind === 'REUSE_CANDIDATE');
608:   const dependencyNodes = nodes.filter((node) => node.kind === 'DEPENDENCY');
609:   const findingKinds = new Set(['REUSE_CANDIDATE', 'PATTERN', 'CONSTRAINT']);
610:   const findingNodes = nodes.filter((node) => findingKinds.has(node.kind));
611: 
612:   const coveredSliceIds = new Set(
613:     edges.filter((edge) => edge.relation === 'COVERED_BY').map((edge) => edge.sourceId),
614:   );
615:   const confirmCountById = new Map<string, number>();
616:   for (const edge of edges) {
617:     if (edge.relation === 'CONFIRMS') {
618:       confirmCountById.set(edge.targetId, (confirmCountById.get(edge.targetId) ?? 0) + 1);
619:     }
620:   }
621:   // DEPENDS_ON is SYMBOL -> DEPENDENCY (the DEPENDENCY node is the edge TARGET), so a
622:   // dependency is "resolved" when it is the target of a DEPENDS_ON edge. IMPORTS is
623:   // FILE -> FILE and never has a DEPENDENCY endpoint, so it is excluded here.
624:   const resolvedDependencyIds = new Set(
625:     edges
626:       .filter((edge) => edge.relation === 'DEPENDS_ON')
627:       .map((edge) => edge.targetId),
628:   );
629: 
630:   const sliceCoverage = sliceNodes.length > 0
631:     ? sliceNodes.filter((node) => coveredSliceIds.has(node.id)).length / sliceNodes.length
632:     : 1;
633:   const reuseCatalogCoverage = reuseNodes.length > 0
634:     ? reuseNodes.filter((node) => contextAgreement(node, confirmCountById) >= 1
635:         || (contextMeta(node) as { verified?: unknown }).verified === true).length / reuseNodes.length
636:     : 1;
637:   // agreementRate is measured over RELEVANCE-GATED findings only: below-gate noise
638:   // is not real context and must not depress cross-executor agreement. relevanceFloor
639:   // separately tracks how much of the catalog clears the gate.
640:   const gatedFindingNodes = findingNodes.filter((node) => contextRelevance(node) >= CONTEXT_RELEVANCE_GATE);
641:   const agreementRate = gatedFindingNodes.length > 0
642:     ? gatedFindingNodes.filter((node) => contextAgreement(node, confirmCountById) >= CONTEXT_AGREEMENT_MIN).length / gatedFindingNodes.length
643:     : 1;
644:   const relevanceFloor = findingNodes.length > 0
645:     ? findingNodes.filter((node) => contextRelevance(node) >= CONTEXT_RELEVANCE_GATE).length / findingNodes.length
646:     : 1;
647:   const dependencyCompleteness = dependencyNodes.length > 0
648:     ? dependencyNodes.filter((node) => resolvedDependencyIds.has(node.id)).length / dependencyNodes.length
649:     : 1;
650: 
651:   return { sliceCoverage, reuseCatalogCoverage, agreementRate, relevanceFloor, dependencyCompleteness };
652: }
653: 
654: /**
655:  * Compute context convergence signals for a namespace.
656:  *
657:  * @param ns - Namespace identifying the coverage graph.
658:  * @returns Context convergence signals with all five metrics.
659:  */
660: export function computeContextSignals(ns: Namespace): ContextConvergenceSignals {
661:   return computeContextSignalsFromData(getNodes(ns), getEdges(ns));
662: }
663: 
664: // ───── EXPORTS ─────
665: 
666: /**
667:  * Compute all convergence signals for a namespace.
668:  *
669:  * Dispatches to research or review signal computation based on loop type.
670:  *
671:  * @param ns - Namespace identifying the coverage graph.
672:  * @returns Research or review convergence signals.
673:  */
674: export function computeSignals(ns: Namespace): ConvergenceSignals {
675:   if (ns.loopType === 'research') {
676:     return computeResearchSignals(ns);
677:   }
678:   if (ns.loopType === 'context') {
679:     return computeContextSignals(ns);
680:   }
681:   return computeReviewSignals(ns);
682: }
683: 
684: /**
685:  * Create a signal snapshot for a given iteration.
686:  *
687:  * Computes convergence signals and node-level signals, then persists
688:  * them via the coverage graph database.
689:  *
690:  * @param ns - Namespace identifying the coverage graph.
691:  * @param iteration - Current iteration number.
692:  * @returns Signal snapshot with signals, node signals, and counts.
693:  */
694: export function createSignalSnapshot(ns: Namespace, iteration: number): SignalSnapshot {
695:   const signals = computeSignals(ns);
696:   const nodeSignals = computeNodeSignals(ns);
697:   const stats = getStats(ns.specFolder, ns.loopType);
698: 
699:   const snapshot: SignalSnapshot = {
700:     iteration,
701:     signals,
702:     nodeSignals,
703:     nodeCount: stats.totalNodes,
704:     edgeCount: stats.totalEdges,
705:   };
706: 
707:   createSnapshot({
708:     specFolder: ns.specFolder,
709:     loopType: ns.loopType,
710:     sessionId: ns.sessionId ?? 'legacy',
711:     iteration,
712:     metrics: {
713:       ...signals,
714:       nodeCount: stats.totalNodes,
715:       edgeCount: stats.totalEdges,
716:     },
717:     nodeCount: stats.totalNodes,
718:     edgeCount: stats.totalEdges,
719:   });
720: 
721:   return snapshot;
722: }
723: 
724: /**
725:  * Compute momentum (change rate) between the latest and previous snapshots.
726:  *
727:  * @param specFolder - Spec folder for namespace scoping.
728:  * @param loopType - Loop type ('research' or 'review').
729:  * @param sessionId - Optional session ID for scoping.
730:  * @returns Map of metric names to deltas, or null if fewer than 2 snapshots.
731:  */
732: export function computeMomentum(
733:   specFolder: string,
734:   loopType: LoopType,
735:   sessionId?: string,
736: ): Record<string, number> | null {
737:   const snapshots = getSnapshots(specFolder, loopType, sessionId);
738:   if (snapshots.length < 2) return null;
739: 
740:   const latest = snapshots[snapshots.length - 1]?.metrics ?? {};
741:   const previous = snapshots[snapshots.length - 2]?.metrics ?? {};
742: 
743:   const momentum: Record<string, number> = {};
744:   for (const key of Object.keys(latest)) {
745:     if (typeof latest[key] === 'number' && typeof previous[key] === 'number') {
746:       momentum[key] = latest[key] - previous[key];
747:     }
748:   }
749: 
750:   return momentum;
751: }

(End of file - total 751 lines)
</content>

1: // MODULE: Deep-Loop Bayesian Scorer
2: 
3: // ───── CORE LOGIC ─────
4: 
5: /**
6:  * Compute a Bayesian estimate of success probability using Laplace smoothing.
7:  *
8:  * @param success - Number of successful outcomes (non-negative integer).
9:  * @param total - Total number of trials (non-negative integer, >= success).
10:  * @returns Smoothed probability: (success + 1) / (total + 2).
11:  * @throws RangeError If inputs are not valid integers or success exceeds total.
12:  */
13: export function computeScore(success: number, total: number): number {
14:   if (!Number.isInteger(success) || !Number.isInteger(total)) {
15:     throw new RangeError('success and total must be integers');
16:   }
17:   if (success < 0 || total < 0) {
18:     throw new RangeError('success and total must be non-negative');
19:   }
20:   if (success > total) {
21:     throw new RangeError('success cannot exceed total');
22:   }
23: 
24:   return (success + 1) / (total + 2);
25: }
26: 
27: /**
28:  * Determine whether a model should be demoted based on score and call count.
29:  *
30:  * @param score - Current Bayesian score (finite number).
31:  * @param totalCalls - Number of calls involving this model (non-negative integer).
32:  * @returns True if score < 0.5 and totalCalls >= 3.
33:  * @throws RangeError If inputs are invalid.
34:  */
35: export function shouldDemote(score: number, totalCalls: number): boolean {
36:   if (!Number.isFinite(score)) {
37:     throw new RangeError('score must be finite');
38:   }
39:   if (!Number.isInteger(totalCalls) || totalCalls < 0) {
40:     throw new RangeError('totalCalls must be a non-negative integer');
41:   }
42: 
43:   return score < 0.5 && totalCalls >= 3;
44: }

13: # Deep Loop Runtime Script Interface Contract
14: 
15: Formal contract for the direct `.cjs` script entry points under `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/`.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: The 118 FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP arc removed the four `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` tools. Their live replacement is direct process invocation through four `.cjs` scripts.
22: 
23: | Script | Replaces | Purpose |
24: |---|---|---|
25: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes graph-aware continue or stop decisions. |
26: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores graph nodes and edges. |
27: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Reads graph views. |
28: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Reports graph health. |
29: 
30: Every script follows the same lifecycle: parse CLI args, re-exec through the TSX loader when needed, import the TypeScript library, open SQLite through `coverage-graph-db.ts`, execute, emit one JSON object to stdout, close DB in `finally`, and map failures to exit codes.
31: 
32: ---
33: 
34: ## 2. SHARED PROCESS CONTRACT
35: 
36: ### Loader Contract
37: 
38: - Scripts are Node executables with `#!/usr/bin/env node`.
39: - Line 2 is `'use strict';`.
40: - Scripts resolve `system-spec-kit/scripts/node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs`.
41: - If `DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED !== '1'`, the script spawns Node with `--import <tsx-loader>` and original args.
42: - The child receives cwd, env plus `DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED=1`, and stdin when stdin is not a TTY.
43: 
44: ### Stdout Contract
45: 
46: - Stdout is JSON-only for workflow-facing responses.
47: - Success uses `status: "ok"`.
48: - Failure uses `status: "error"`, `error`, and `code`.
49: - Script errors may write diagnostic stack JSON to stderr.
50: - Workflow YAML should parse stdout, not stderr.
51: 
52: ### Exit-Code Matrix
53: 
54: | Exit code | Meaning | Source condition |
55: |---:|---|---|
56: | 0 | OK | Valid input and successful operation. |
57: | 1 | Script error | Unexpected runtime failure or uncategorized exception. |
58: | 2 | DB error | SQLite or DB lifecycle failure. |
59: | 3 | Input validation error | Missing args, invalid loop type, invalid query type, malformed JSON, or unexpected positional args. |
60: 
61: ### Required Namespace Args
62: 
63: | Arg | Required | Values | Notes |
64: |---|---|---|---|
65: | `--spec-folder` | Yes | path string | Stored as `specFolder`. |
66: | `--loop-type` | Yes | `research` or `review` | Validated before DB operations. |
67: | `--session-id` | Yes | string | Scopes graph reads and writes to one loop lineage. |
68: 
69: ---
70: 
71: ## 3. SCRIPT CONTRACTS
72: 
73: ### convergence.cjs
74: 
75: Source: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs`
76: 
77: Additional args: `--iteration`, `--persist-snapshot`.
78: 
79: Workflow-facing fields: `graph_decision`, `graph_signals_json`, `graph_blockers_json`, `graph_stop_blocked`, `graph_convergence_score`.
80: 
81: Behavior: Returns `CONTINUE` for empty graphs, `STOP_BLOCKED` when blocking guards fail, and `STOP_ALLOWED` when all convergence signals pass.
82: 
83: ### upsert.cjs
84: 
85: Source: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs`
86: 
87: Additional args: `--nodes`, `--edges`, or `--events`.
88: 
89: Workflow-facing fields: `graph_nodes_json`, `graph_edges_json`, `graph_upsert_event_count`.
90: 
91: Behavior: Requires at least one node or edge, validates kinds and relations by loop type, rejects self-loops, and clamps weights.
92: 
93: ### query.cjs
94: 
95: Source: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/query.cjs`
96: 
97: Additional args: `--query-type`, optional `--limit`, `--node-id`, `--max-depth`.
98: 
99: Workflow-facing fields: `data.queryType`, `data.namespace`, query-specific arrays.
100: 
101: Behavior: Supports uncovered questions, coverage gaps, unverified claims, contradictions, provenance chains, and hot nodes.
102: 
103: ### status.cjs
104: 
105: Source: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/status.cjs`
106: 
107: Additional args: none beyond namespace args.
108: 
109: Workflow-facing fields: `schemaVersion`, `rowCount`, counts, breakdowns, signals.
110: 
111: Behavior: Reports clean empty state with null signals when no nodes exist.
112: 
113: ---
114: 
115: ## 4. DB LIFECYCLE REQUIREMENT
116: 
117: Scripts must not open SQLite directly. They import `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts`, rely on lazy `getDb()` or library functions, and call `db.closeDb()` inside `finally`.
118: 
119: Visible anchors:
120: 
121: - `scripts/convergence.cjs` closes after signal and query work.
122: - `scripts/upsert.cjs` closes after `batchUpsert`.
123: - `scripts/query.cjs` closes after query dispatch.
124: - `scripts/status.cjs` closes after status assembly.
125: - `tests/lifecycle/db-open-close.vitest.ts` covers lifecycle behavior.
126: 
127: ---
128: 
129: ## 5. PERMISSIONS-GATE RELATIONSHIP
130: 
131: `permissions-gate` is not called directly by the scripts. It is part of the pre-dispatch runtime safety layer for loop orchestration. Script execution is an `execute` operation that should be allowed only for runtime-owned script paths in any active permission matrix.
132: 
133: Relevant source:
134: 
135: - `lib/deep-loop/permissions-gate.ts` maps tools and bash commands to operation classes.
136: - `SKILL.md` states direct `.cjs` invocation is the supported consumer path.
137: - `/doctor deep-loop` marks the DB route as mutating before snapshots or repair.
138: 
139: ---
140: 
141: ## 6. FAILURE TRIAGE
142: 
143: | Symptom | First check | Likely fix |
144: |---|---|---|
145: | Exit 3 | Missing namespace arg or malformed JSON | Correct CLI args or JSON quoting. |
146: | Exit 2 | SQLite open or query failure | Inspect schema version and DB path. |
147: | Exit 1 | Unexpected exception | Read stderr stack JSON, then inspect the called library. |
148: | Non-JSON stdout | Script contract drift | Restore JSON-only stdout or update YAML output bindings. |
149: | Empty convergence output | Graph has zero nodes | Upsert graph events before convergence or accept `CONTINUE`. |
150: 
151: ---
152: 
153: ## 7. SOURCE ANCHORS
154: 
155: | Path | Contract Role |
156: |---|---|
157: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | Stop-decision script and convergence output bindings. |
158: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | Mutation script and graph event ingestion. |
159: | `scripts/query.cjs` | Read script and query-type dispatch. |
160: | `scripts/status.cjs` | Health script and schema/count reporting. |
161: | `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` | DB lifecycle owner. |
162: | `lib/deep-loop/permissions-gate.ts` | Pre-dispatch execute/write boundary. |
163: | `tests/integration/*-script.vitest.ts` | Script-level regression coverage. |
164: | `tests/lifecycle/db-open-close.vitest.ts` | DB lifecycle regression coverage. |
165: 

(End of file - total 165 lines)
</content>
